title: Rheumatoid Arthritis: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Rheumatoid Arthritis
Identify key diagnostic features of Rheumatoid Arthritis
Apply clinical reasoning to Rheumatoid Arthritis management
Recognize common pitfalls in Rheumatoid Arthritis

sections.title: Overview of Rheumatoid Arthritis
sections.content:
**Rheumatoid Arthritis**

Symmetric small joint involvement, RF vs anti-CCP, pannus formation, DMARDs, extra-articular manifestations

**Key Points:**
• Symmetric inflammatory polyarthritis affecting MCP and PIP joints (spares DIP — that's OA)
• Morning stiffness >1 hour (vs OA: <30 minutes, improves with rest)
• Anti-CCP: most specific; RF: sensitive but less specific (present in many conditions)

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Symmetric inflammatory polyarthritis affecting MCP and PIP joints (spares DIP — that's OA)
• Morning stiffness >1 hour (vs OA: <30 minutes, improves with rest)

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Anti-CCP: most specific; RF: sensitive but less specific (present in many conditions)
• Pannus formation: synovial proliferation → cartilage/bone erosion
• Treatment: early DMARDs (methotrexate first-line), biologics (anti-TNF) if inadequate response

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Rheumatoid Arthritis:**

• Anti-CCP: most specific; RF: sensitive but less specific (present in many conditions)
• Pannus formation: synovial proliferation → cartilage/bone erosion
• Treatment: early DMARDs (methotrexate first-line), biologics (anti-TNF) if inadequate response

options.explanation: Symmetric inflammatory polyarthritis affecting MCP and PIP joints (spares DIP — that's OA)

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Symmetric inflammatory polyarthritis affecting MCP and PIP joints (spares DIP — that's OA).
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Symmetric inflammatory polyarthritis affecting MCP and PIP joints (spares DIP — that's OA)

coachDx.misconception: While typically self-resolving without treatment may seem plausible, it does not match the clinical scenario described.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Symmetric inflammatory polyarthritis affecting MCP and PIP joints (spares DIP —  from Typically self-resolving without treatment.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Symmetric inflammatory polyarthritis affecting MCP and PIP joints (spares DIP — that's OA).

mcqs.stem: A 65-year-old woman with new-onset headache, jaw claudication, and ESR of 85 mm/hr is suspected of having giant cell arteritis. What is the most appropriate immediate action?

options.text: Order CRP and IL-6 levels before making treatment decisions
options.explanation: While CRP and IL-6 are elevated in GCA, additional testing should not delay treatment in a patient with classic symptoms and elevated ESR. Vision loss can occur within hours.

options.text: Schedule temporal artery biopsy first to confirm diagnosis before starting treatment
options.explanation: Waiting for biopsy before treatment risks permanent vision loss. Biopsy can be performed within 2 weeks of starting steroids without significantly affecting sensitivity.

options.text: Start low-dose prednisone (10 mg/day) and monitor symptoms for 1 week
options.explanation: Low-dose steroids are insufficient for GCA. The recommended dose is 40-60 mg/day for uncomplicated GCA, or 1 mg/kg/day (up to IV pulse methylprednisolone 1g × 3 days) when visual symptoms are present.

options.text: Start high-dose prednisone (1 mg/kg/day) immediately, then arrange temporal artery biopsy
options.explanation: Per ACR/EULAR guidelines, GCA with visual symptoms is an emergency. High-dose glucocorticoids must be started IMMEDIATELY to prevent irreversible vision loss. Temporal artery biopsy remains diagnostic even after several weeks of steroid therapy due to persistent inflammatory changes.

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Start high-dose prednisone (1 mg/kg/day) immediately, then arrange temporal artery biopsy.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Start high-dose prednisone (1 mg/kg/day) immediately, then arrange temporal artery biopsy

coachDx.misconception: While CRP and IL-6 are elevated in GCA, additional testing should not delay treatment in a patient with classic symptoms and elevated ESR. Vision loss can occur within hours.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Start high-dose prednisone (1 mg/kg/day) immediately, then arrange temporal arte from Order CRP and IL-6 levels before making treatment decisions.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Start high-dose prednisone (1 mg/kg/day) immediately, then arrange temporal artery biopsy.
